commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR enables Telegram undici TCP keepalive, changes polling-start status patches to preserve `connected`, updates polling tests, and widens shared channel connect grace to 300s.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced regression: source inspection shows a prior `connected:true` can survive restart, have transport timestamps cleared, and then pass health after grace if no first `getUpdates` succeeds. The original NAT-expiry stall was not independently reproduced in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers are narrow Telegram status and diagnostic repairs that an automated worker can attempt on the PR branch with focused regression coverage.

target.title: fix(telegram): prevent polling stalls via TCP keepalive and widened connect grace
target.body:
## Summary

Two complementary fixes for Telegram polling stalls observed in production where the long-poll `getUpdates` connection silently dies and the gateway either hangs until the stall watchdog fires (~90s) or self-flags `disconnected` during a healthy poll-cycle restart.

### 1. TCP keepalive on getUpdates (`extensions/telegram/src/fetch.ts`)

**Root cause:** Long-poll connections to `api.telegram.org` stay idle for up to the `getUpdates` timeout (~900s). Most home/office NAT tables expire idle TCP entries after 60-1800s (commonly ~1000s). When the NAT entry is silently dropped the TCP connection hangs rather than returning an error, leaving the grammY runner stuck until the 90s stall watchdog fires and forces a restart cycle.

**Fix:** Unconditionally set `keepAlive: true` and `keepAliveInitialDelay: 30_000` (30s) on the undici Agent `connect` options built in `buildTelegramConnectOptions`. OS-level TCP keepalive probes sent every ~75s (OS default) will:
1. Refresh the NAT table entry before it expires.
2. Surface dead connections immediately with `ETIMEDOUT` instead of hanging forever.

This is the transport-layer prevention complement to upstream's existing detection-layer work (#78646 watchdog, #81746 event-loop stall detection, #82256 spool recovery, #82227 outbound drain). Detection mechanisms still catch any failure mode that escapes the keepalive; this PR cuts the volume of stalls that those mechanisms have to handle.

### 2. Stop self-flagging disconnected on poll-cycle start; widen channel connect grace to 300s

**Root cause:**
- `notePollingStart` was unconditionally setting `connected: false` at the start of every poll cycle, including legitimate reconnect cycles where polling never actually dropped. The downstream health monitor then saw an apparent disconnect and could trigger an unnecessary restart.
- `DEFAULT_CHANNEL_CONNECT_GRACE_MS` was 120s. Slow-startup channels (e.g. bots with many commands, large first-page payloads) finish the full handshake after 2-3 minutes. The health monitor evaluated them as `disconnected` before they finished connecting, causing premature restart cycles.

**Fix:**
- `polling-status.ts`: `notePollingStart` no longer stomps the `connected` flag. Only `notePollingStop` writes `connected: false`. Telemetry fields (lastConnectedAt, lastEventAt, lastTransportActivityAt) are reset at cycle start so the health monitor sees fresh state.
- `channel-health-policy.ts`: `DEFAULT_CHANNEL_CONNECT_GRACE_MS` widened from 120_000 to 300_000. This is in core (shared across channels) because the underlying property — long handshake time before stable connection — is not telegram-specific. Any channel with a slow first connect benefits.

## Test plan

- [x] Verified TCP keepalive activates: `keepAlive: true` confirmed in undici Agent connect options via existing `polling-fetch.test.ts` assertions on the Agent config.
- [x] `polling-status.test.ts` updated: covers the new `notePollingStart`-does-not-stomp-connected behavior and the cycle-start telemetry reset.
- [x] `polling-session.test.ts` updated: covers the resulting single disconnect patch (was two) when a stall-and-recover cycle runs.
- [x] `channel-health-policy.ts`: constant change only, existing tests unchanged.
- [x] Verified stable in production on two hosts (mac-mini, rh-bot) over 3+ days with zero telegram polling stalls.

## Diagnosis signature this PR resolves

`~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` repeated entries of the form:

```
[telegram] [diag] polling cycle error reason=getUpdates ... err=Call to 'getUpdates' failed! ... timeout
[fetch-timeout] fetch timeout after 10000ms ... operation=fetchWithTimeout url=https://api.telegram.org/.../getMe
```

paired with `gateway-health-monitor` restarting the telegram channel due to a "disconnected" reading shortly after a normal poll-cycle start.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram long-poll `getUpdates` connection hangs silently after NAT-table entry expires; gateway either waits ~90s for the in-process stall watchdog or self-flags `disconnected` during a legitimate poll-cycle restart, triggering unnecessary channel restart cycles.
- **Real environment tested**: 2 production gateway hosts running this branch as part of `upgrade-2026.5.12` (which carries both commits): `mac-mini.lan` (Kbot / @Kebablebot + @RattyMcRatfaceBot) and `rh-bot.lan` (Requesty / @requestymcrequestfacebot). macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node 22+, undici via grammY runner long-poll.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  # On each host:
  openclaw gateway status            # confirm 2026.5.12 runtime
  openclaw channels status           # confirm telegram polling status + last-activity timestamps
  ```
- **Evidence after fix (live terminal output, mac-mini.lan, 2026-05-18)**:
  ```
  CLI version: 2026.5.12 (/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw)
  Gateway version: 2026.5.12
  Runtime: running (pid 50650, state active)
  Connectivity probe: ok
  - Telegram default: enabled, configured, running, connected, in:12h ago, out:16m ago, mode:polling, token:config, groups:unmentioned
  - Telegram ratbot: enabled, configured, running, connected, in:28h ago, out:28h ago, mode:polling, token:config, groups:unmentioned
  ```
  Same gateway pid (50650) has now held for over 24h continuously, with telegram outbound activity within the last 16 minutes for the default account. Pre-fix observation log signature `[fetch-timeout] fetch timeout after 10000ms ... operation=fetchWithTimeout url=https://api.telegram.org/...` no longer appears in `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.err.log` post-fix on either host.
- **Observed result after fix**: Both gateways' telegram channels report `running, connected` continuously since deploy; no `[telegram] [diag] polling cycle error reason=getUpdates ... err=...timeout` repeats; no health-monitor-initiated channel restarts; runtime uptime measured in days not minutes.
- **What was not tested**: Behavior under deliberately-induced NAT entry expiry (would need a NAT/router scenario I can't reproduce on the LAN). Behavior under a host with `KeepAlive` already disabled at the OS level — undici docs imply OS default kicks in regardless, but I haven't verified that path explicitly.
